change_comments.message:
Results look much better now. As far as I can tell, only `policy-clamp-ac-k8s-ppnt` [still has `GroupAuthorizationException`s](https://logs.onap.org/onap-integration/gating/143337-8/infrastructure-healthcheck/k8s-teardown/k8s-teardown/pod-onap-policy-clamp-ac-k8s-ppnt-5478c66d9b-z7lst-policy-clamp-ac-k8s-ppnt-logs.html):

```
[2026-02-27T12:19:00.894+00:00|INFO|ConsumerCoordinator|KAFKA-source-acm-ppnt-sync] [Consumer clientId=consumer-1d8f5214-b321-4133-aed4-9d918d6e6020-3, groupId=1d8f5214-b321-4133-aed4-9d918d6e6020] FindCoordinator request hit fatal exception
org.apache.kafka.common.errors.GroupAuthorizationException: Not authorized to access group: 1d8f5214-b321-4133-aed4-9d918d6e6020
[2026-02-27T12:19:00.894+00:00|ERROR|SingleThreadedBusTopicSource|KAFKA-source-acm-ppnt-sync] SingleThreadedKafkaTopicSource [getTopicCommInfrastructure()=KAFKA, toString()=SingleThreadedBusTopicSource [consumerGroup=1d8f5214-b321-4133-aed4-9d918d6e6020, consumerInstance=onap-policy-clamp-ac-k8s-ppnt-5478c66d9b-z7lst, fetchTimeout=15000, fetchLimit=-1, consumer=KafkaConsumerWrapper [fetchTimeout=15000], alive=true, locked=false, uebThread=Thread[KAFKA-source-acm-ppnt-sync,5,main], topicListeners=1, toString()=BusTopicBase [apiKey=null, apiSecret=null, useHttps=false, allowSelfSignedCerts=false, toString()=TopicBase [servers=[onap-strimzi-kafka-bootstrap:9092], topic=acm-ppnt-sync, effectiveTopic=acm-ppnt-sync, #recentEvents=0, locked=false, #topicListeners=1]]]]: cannot fetch
org.apache.kafka.common.errors.GroupAuthorizationException: Not authorized to access group: 1d8f5214-b321-4133-aed4-9d918d6e6020
```
Also when you rebase, the Jobbuilder failure will go away

inline_comments.message:
The pod fails to start because the file that you are pointing spring to here does not exist:
```
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt                                                                                                                       │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt [2026-02-21T16:21:40.833+00:00|ERROR|LoggingFailureAnalysisReporter|main]                                             │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt                                                                                                                       │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt ***************************                                                                                           │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt APPLICATION FAILED TO START                                                                                           │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt ***************************                                                                                           │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt                                                                                                                       │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt Description:                                                                                                          │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt                                                                                                                       │
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt Config data resource 'file [/opt/app/policy/clamp/etc/mounted/application.yaml]' via location '/opt/app/policy/clamp/ │
│ etc/mounted/application.yaml' does not exist
```

That is because you have made the file conditional in the configmap with:
```
{{- if .Values.a1pmsconfig }}
```
and the values are ultimately not defined in the values.yaml.

I see two options to fix that:
- wrap the `SPRING_CONFIG_LOCATION` declaration here in a `{{- if .Values.a1pmsconfig }}` as well
- define `.Values.a1pmsconfig` in the values yaml

Typo `vale`.

Which leads to the pod log being full of `Invalid value` lines:

```
policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt [2026-02-25T14:15:46.589+00:00|ERROR|KafkaHealthCheck|main] Invalid value  for configuration security.protocol: String must be one of (case insensitive): SASL_SSL, PLAINTEXT, SSL, SASL_PLAINTEXT
│ policy-clamp-ac-a1pms-ppnt [2026-02-25T14:16:01.590+00:00|ERROR|KafkaHealthCheck|main] Invalid value  for configuration security.protocol: String must be one of (case insensitive): SASL_SSL, PLAINTEXT, SSL, SASL_PLAINTEX
│ 
```

You forgot to add the new entries under `volumeMounts` and `volumes` that you declared for a1pms also for the other participants.

Thats why `ac-http-ppnt`, `ac-kserve-ppnt`, `ac-pf-ppnt` are in `CrashLoopBackOff` state
Please undo this change. Then it should work (pods are crashing)
